# profit-pdf-generator — Full Details

**Parent:** `profit-pdf-generator/SKILL.md`

**Auto-generated by Context Diet:** 2026-03-13

**This file contains the full specification. SKILL.md is the routing card.**


### OUTPUT 1: PROFIT PDF DRAFT (5-10 pages, Canva/Gamma ready)

Generate the complete PDF content using this exact structure:

```
PAGE 1 — TITLE PAGE
[Main Title — punchy, benefit-driven, 8 words max]
[Subtitle — who it's for + what result they'll get]
[One-liner: "A free guide for [target client] who want to [result] without [pain point]."]

PAGE 2 — INTRODUCTION
[Call out the reader — "If you're a [target client] who..."]
[State the main problem they face — be specific and empathetic]
[Promise the result — "In this guide, I'm going to show you exactly how to..."]
[Quick credibility line — "I've helped [X] people / I personally [result]"]

PAGES 3-7 — MAIN CONTENT (3-5 sections)

Section 1: [Clear heading]
[Explain the concept in simple, direct language]
[Give 3-5 practical, actionable steps or tips]
[Include placeholder: [INSERT CLIENT RESULT / BEFORE-AFTER HERE]]

>> Mini CTA: "Want help applying this to your specific situation? [CTA action] — [CTA link placeholder]."

Section 2: [Clear heading]
[Same structure as above]
[INSERT PROOF PLACEHOLDER]

>> Mini CTA: "Ready to stop guessing and get a plan that works for you? [CTA action]."

Section 3: [Clear heading]
[Same structure]
[INSERT PROOF PLACEHOLDER]

>> Mini CTA: "This is exactly what I help my clients with. [CTA action]."

[Section 4-5 if needed — same structure]

PAGE 8-9 — PROOF PAGE
[Headline: "Here's What Happens When You Apply This"]
[INSERT 3-5 CLIENT RESULT PLACEHOLDERS with format:]
  - [Client Name/Initial]: "[Quote about their result]"
  - [Before/After photo placeholder]
  - [Screenshot of result placeholder]

PAGE 10 — FINAL CTA PAGE
[Headline: "Ready to [Get Result] Without [Pain Point]?"]
[2-3 sentences summarizing what they learned]
[Bold CTA: "If you want my help implementing this step by step so you get results faster, [CTA ACTION]. Click the link below or DM me '[KEYWORD]' and I'll send you the details."]
[CTA LINK PLACEHOLDER]
[P.S. line: "P.S. — The people who get the best results are the ones who take action today, not next week. [CTA action] now."]
```

**Style rules for the PDF draft:**
- Direct, conversational tone. Write like you're texting a friend who asked for help.
- Short sentences. Short paragraphs. No walls of text.
- No em dashes. Use periods or commas instead.
- Bold key phrases for scanning.
- Each section should be actionable — the reader should be able to DO something after each section.
- Do NOT gatekeep. Give real, valuable advice. The PDF should be genuinely useful.
- Include specific numbers, examples, and scenarios wherever possible.

### OUTPUT 4: DM SALES SCRIPT

```
DM SALES SCRIPT — "The Natural Conversation"

GOAL: Book a Zoom call. That's it. Don't sell coaching in the DMs.

STEP 1 — OPEN (after they receive the PDF)
"Hey [Name]! Saw you grabbed the [PDF Title]. Love that. What made you want to check it out?"

STEP 2 — DIAGNOSE (understand their situation)
"That makes sense. So are you currently [doing the thing] or is this more of a 'I want to start' situation?"
[Listen. Ask 1-2 follow-up questions about their specific situation.]

STEP 3 — IDENTIFY THE GAP
"Got it. So it sounds like you know WHAT to do, but the challenge is [their specific struggle]. Is that right?"
[Wait for confirmation.]

STEP 4 — BRIDGE TO CALL
"Makes total sense. That's actually the exact thing I help people with in my coaching program. I do a quick free call where I look at your specific situation and give you a game plan. Would that be helpful?"

STEP 5 — BOOK
"Awesome. Here's my calendar link: [LINK]. Grab whatever time works best. Talk soon!"

RULES:
- Never pitch coaching unless they express a desire for help.
- Never send walls of text. Match their energy and message length.
- Ask questions. Don't lecture.
- The DM conversation should feel like two people talking, not a sales script.
- If they're not ready, don't push. Say: "No worries at all. The PDF has everything you need to get started. Feel free to reach out anytime."
```

### OUTPUT 6: VOLUME GAME PROJECTIONS

```
VOLUME GAME CALCULATOR

Your numbers (adjust based on your situation):

Posts per week:          [X]
Avg views per post:     [estimate or ask]
Keyword comment rate:   ~1-3% of viewers
DMs per month:          [calculated]
DM-to-call rate:        ~4% (industry benchmark)
Calls booked/month:     [calculated]
Close rate:             ~25-50% (depending on offer price)
Revenue per client:     $[X]

PROJECTED MONTHLY REVENUE:
Conservative (1%): [X] views x 1% = [Y] DMs x 4% = [Z] calls x 25% close x $[price] = $[revenue]
Moderate (2%):     [X] views x 2% = [Y] DMs x 4% = [Z] calls x 35% close x $[price] = $[revenue]
Aggressive (3%):   [X] views x 3% = [Y] DMs x 4% = [Z] calls x 50% close x $[price] = $[revenue]

THE VOLUME LEVER:
To get [target] calls/month, you need [X] DMs.
To get [X] DMs, you need [Y] keyword comments.
To get [Y] comments, you need [Z] views.
To get [Z] views, post [N] times per week with strong hooks.

WHAT ACTUALLY MOVES THE NEEDLE:
1. Better hooks = more views = more DMs (highest leverage)
2. More proof in content = higher trust = higher DM-to-call rate
3. CTAs on EVERYTHING = more keyword triggers
4. Daily Stories = constant reminder = compounds over time
```
